pr.title: Allow split_suggester tool to interpret new labels
pr.author: DRGN-DRC

summary: Updated tools/split_suggester.py for the repository's newer float-label style after float labels dropped the lbl_ prefix. The tool now detects sdata references via @sda instead of searching for lbl_, supports pulling function names from build/map.csv by default through parse_map, adds a --noNames option to skip name lookup, removes mapNames as a global by passing it explicitly, and improves map parsing/error handling.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; updated label parsing, map/map.csv name lookup, CLI options, and error handling.

what_changed: Changed float/sdata label detection in assembly parsing from checking for the literal substring "lbl_" to checking for "@sda", allowing labels without the old lbl_ prefix to be interpreted.
Added import of parse_map and pathlib.Path so split_suggester can generate and parse ./build/map.csv when no explicit .map file is supplied.
Added parseMapCsv(), which calls parse_map.main(), reads build/map.csv, and maps function virtual start addresses to function names.
Changed function-name lookup flow: --noNames disables lookup, -m/--mapFile parses a supplied .map file, otherwise the tool now defaults to map.csv lookup.
Added -nn/--noNames command-line argument for suppressing .map and map.csv name lookup.
Removed mapNames as implicit global state by passing mapNames into Function.checkForMapName(), parseAssemblyFile(), and printResults().
Adjusted .map parsing to wait until the ".text section layout" section and parse function start/name from later fields rather than the old first/last-field layout.
Documented --consoleOnly, --noNames/map.csv behavior, and exit code 4 in the script header.
Changed top-level exit calls to sys.exit and added a generic exception handler that prints the error and exits with code 4.
Expanded some parse error reporting to print caught exceptions, and clarified a folder creation error message.
smart_moves: Using "@sda" instead of "lbl_" ties float-reference detection to the assembly relocation/small-data syntax rather than to a naming prefix that had changed.
Defaulting to build/map.csv via parse_map lets the tool use the repository's current generated map data without requiring users to manually pass a .map file every run.
Adding --noNames preserves a fast/offline escape hatch for users who do not want map.csv generation or name lookup.
Passing mapNames explicitly removes hidden global coupling and makes parsing/name lookup behavior easier to reason about.
Restricting .map parsing to the ".text section layout" section reduces accidental parsing of unrelated map-file lines.
decomp_lessons: Decomp tooling should avoid depending on temporary label prefixes like lbl_ when the assembly syntax contains a more semantic marker such as @sda.
When repository naming conventions change, helper tools that parse generated assembly need updates just as much as source or headers do.
Generated project metadata such as build/map.csv can be a better integration point for tooling than ad hoc manual map-file paths, but tools should still offer an opt-out.
Avoid globals for shared parse data such as function-name maps; pass them through call chains so behavior is explicit and easier to test.
Map-file parsers should anchor to known section headers and field positions rather than assuming every non-empty line has the desired layout.

naming_conventions.lesson: The PR body states the repo had renamed float labels, dropping the "lbl_" prefix; split_suggester was updated accordingly.
Assembly lines referencing small-data labels are now identified by "@sda" rather than by a label-name prefix.
New CLI option name for disabling function-name lookup from both .map files and map.csv.
Existing option is now documented in the header as preventing creation of generated .c templates.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: For detecting float loads from sdata, parse the instruction after the comment delimiter and extract the label before the @ relocation suffix.
Use @sda as the stable signal for small-data references, then still classify float types based on load instruction prefixes such as lfs.
Use function start addresses from map.csv or .map data to annotate generated function headers with better names while filtering names beginning with "zz" or ending with "_" in checkForMapName().
When parsing map.csv, convert the numeric function address field to uppercase hexadecimal without the 0x prefix to match the address key format used elsewhere in split_suggester.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching tools/split_suggester.py to see whether @sda parsing or map.csv integration needed additional fixes.
Search for other tooling that still detects labels using the old lbl_ prefix.
Review parse_map and build/map.csv format changes around late 2023 to confirm field assumptions used by parseMapCsv.
Check whether split_suggester error handling was later refined around ProgramError args and generic exit code 4.
